channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Motorists Association Opposes NTSA Proposal to Charge for E-Logbook Conversion, Calling It Double Taxation
item.description: The Motorists Association of Kenya (MAK) has strongly opposed the National Transport and Safety Authority’s (NTSA) proposal to introduce charges for converting physical vehicle logbooks into electroni...

item.title: Five children killed in two-vehicle collision near Kitchener-Waterloo, OPP say
item.description: Five children have died and several others have been injured following a serious two-vehicle collision in rural Ontario, according to the Ontario Provincial Police (OPP). The crash occurred around 7:3...

item.title: Trump Pollster Warns Declining Support Among Working-Class White Voters Could Cost Republicans Congress
item.description: A report highlighted growing concerns within Republican circles about President Donald Trump's declining support among working-class white voters without college degrees, a demographic that has been c...

item.title: Anwar says Negeri Sembilan snap election was unavoidable amid political tensions with BN
item.description: Malaysia Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim stated that he did not desire a snap state election in Negeri Sembilan but said his Pakatan Harapan (PH) coalition had “no choice” due to political actions by its...

item.title: Political Commentators Speculate About Susie Wiles' Frustration With White House Decisions

item.title: Adam Kinzinger Accuses Trump of Offering Major Financial Concessions to Iran in Pursuit of a Strait of Hormuz Agreement
item.description: Former Republican Congressman Adam Kinzinger publicly criticized President Donald Trump's handling of the conflict and negotiations involving Iran, arguing that the administration is making significan...

item.title: Critics Interpret Trump’s Iran Comment as Possible Nuclear Threat and Renew Calls for Impeachment
item.description: A Raw Story article reports on reactions to a social media post by President Donald Trump regarding ongoing negotiations between the United States and Iran. In the post, Trump expressed hope that dipl...

item.title: Smith College Taught Freshmen Safe Luggage-Handling Techniques After Train Station Porter Services Ended in 1958
item.description: A historical photo feature revisits an unusual physical education class held at Smith College in Northampton, Massachusetts, in 1958. The program emerged after nearby railroad stations reduced or elim...

item.title: Police arrest main suspect in murder of Father Allois Cheruiyot Bett in Kerio Valley investigation
item.description: Kenyan police have arrested the main suspect linked to the killing of Catholic priest Father Allois Cheruiyot Bett, who was shot dead on 22 May 2025 in the Tot area of Kerio Valley after celebrating M...

item.title: Undercover Peruvian Police Use World Cup Mascot Costumes to Arrest Suspected Drug Dealer
item.description: Peruvian police arrested a suspected drug dealer in Lima after carrying out an undercover operation in which two officers disguised themselves as World Cup mascots. According to Colonel Carlos Alcánta...

item.title: Organizations and Democrats Criticize Proposed OMB Rule Expanding Political Oversight of Federal Grants
item.description: More than 300 organizations have urged Office of Management and Budget Director Russell Vought to extend the public comment period for a proposed federal grant rule that would significantly increase p...

item.title: Trump warns of unspecified contingency option if Iran nuclear talks with Tehran collapse
item.description: The article reports on comments made by U.S. President Donald Trump regarding ongoing negotiations between Washington and Tehran over a potential diplomatic agreement. In a post on his social media pl...

item.title: Ratings decline follows CBS late-night programming change after Colbert departure
item.description: CBS’s decision to end Stephen Colbert’s tenure on The Late Show and replace the program with a new late-night arrangement under media entrepreneur Byron Allen has been followed by a significant drop i...

item.title: Former Staffer Questions Barry Moore’s Longstanding Claims About Military Service
item.description: U.S. Representative Barry Moore of Alabama, who is running for the U.S. Senate with the endorsement of President Donald Trump, is facing renewed scrutiny over statements regarding his military backgro...

item.title: Trita Parsi Claims Deportation Allegations Were Based on an Unfounded Report and Prompted Public Support

item.title: Rat Eradication on Palau Island Leads to Rapid Recovery of Seabirds, Fish, and Coral Reefs
item.description: A conservation experiment conducted on Ulong Island in Palau has revealed that ecosystems can recover much faster than previously believed after the removal of invasive rats. The project, led by the n...

item.title: Legal Experts Raise Concerns Over Judicial Nominee Justin Smith Ahead of Senate Confirmation Vote
item.description: Justin D. Smith, one of President Donald Trump's attorneys in the E. Jean Carroll litigation, is nearing confirmation to a lifetime position on the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Eighth Circuit. The Se...

item.title: Royal Family marks King Charles III's official birthday during Trooping the Colour celebrations
item.description: Thousands of spectators gathered in central London for the annual Trooping the Colour ceremony, celebrating the official birthday of King Charles III. The event, one of the most significant occasions ...

item.title: Mike Johnson Signals Republican Interest in Reforming Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security
item.description: A political controversy emerged after House Speaker Mike Johnson discussed federal entitlement programs during an interview with conservative radio host Moon Griffon. Johnson argued that the largest d...

item.title: Israel Proposes Temporary Entry Restrictions for Travellers from Five East African Countries Amid Ebola Concerns
item.description: Israel's Ministry of Health has recommended temporary travel restrictions targeting non-citizens and non-residents who have recently visited five East African countries affected by an Ebola outbreak. ...
